Iran Hardens Nuclear Stance Ahead of US Talks

According to Reuters, Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei has ordered that Iran’s stockpile of highly enriched uranium must remain inside the country, rejecting a key US demand to transfer it abroad.

Iranian officials reportedly fear sending the material abroad would leave the country vulnerable to future attacks.

tweets.title:
🤞
Well, this sounds good as a concept and, in fact, was used by most countries until a few years ago. However, let me tell you industries across the world are now going back to the old-school approach. “Just-in-time” inventory management is gradually being reconsidered, and strategic stockpiling is making a comeback.

In fact, my own team learned this the hard way. A couple of years ago, we were sourcing certain materials from a country that was considered friendly at the time. Then the regime changed, relations turned hostile, and suddenly all three silos of our supply chain got stuck.

We had order books to fulfil, penalty clauses in place, and no immediate workaround. Ironically, the material itself was at the bottom of the value chain if we had enough time, we could have manufactured it ourselves without much difficulty. But timelines don’t wait.

We ended up paying penalties, and since then we’ve been working aggressively to make our supply chain more robust bringing as much as possible within our own sphere of influence. And let me assure you, doing that on the go is far from easy.

Eyup, R&D in everything is expensive and time taking for country like india, with limited budget and time, we should focus on niche technologies to put our existing resources, there R&D is needed where we learn something new. But for tech we already have building blocks, what we know we can develop, it’s not required to make everything on our own when we are getting it from somewhere with tech transfer and local production.
Things like Jet engine tech requires more of our R&D effort than tech we have already mastered.
Everything should be made in india is a myth. You can’t, no country does as we don’t have all the resources you will always have to import things.
It’s better to put context, than a clip so that you don’t create controversy and people understand the point I was making.
You can’t expect Startup to do R&D from the start, it’s hard to get investment, if you want to sustain you need orders then you can focus on R&D.
